query_normalized: quantifying the impact of data resolution on simulated fluid turbulence physics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) and (b) as it investigates the impact of simulation resolution (via "highly refined" meshes in Large Eddy Simulations) on the accuracy of capturing turbulent correlations and near-wall data in fluid flows, which directly addresses the user's interest in how data resolution affects simulated turbulence physics.
